run_metadata.coverage_summary: DEGRADED RUN. Live detail-page fetching was impossible this session: WebFetch (and curl via the agent proxy) returned HTTP 403 on the CONNECT tunnel for EVERY destination host tested — all Tier-1/2/3 tender portals and buyer sites, and even control hosts (example.com, google.com, wikipedia.org). This is an outbound egress-policy denial recorded at the proxy gateway, not per-site bot protection, and per the environment README it must be reported rather than routed around. WebSearch (server-side, not routed through the egress proxy) remained functional. Consequently: (1) Opportunity discovery was limited to identity/headline data that the web-search index surfaces from the live government pages (real deep-link URLs, tender IDs, titles, and snippet-quoted publish/closing dates). No tender detail page or specification document could be opened, so deliverables, requirements, eligibility, evaluation criteria, documents and contacts could NOT be captured for any tender and are set null — every reported tender is HEADLINE-LEVEL ONLY and counted in entries_pending_detail. The extraction method self-corroborated: two search-surfaced items matched exactly against prior fetch-verified runs, giving confidence the deep-link URLs/IDs are genuine rather than fabricated. (2) Source discovery (the second half of the mission) was productive: ~20 genuinely new Australian buyer-originated sources were identified via WebSearch and recorded in discovered_sources. Tier-1 (AusTender, Buying for Victoria) yielded 4 new headline-level ATMs; NSW/QLD/WA/SA/TAS/NT/ACT state portals yielded no clean fresh non-excluded state tender that could be captured with confidence. Tier-3 corporate opportunity detail is login/invitation-gated as expected; no corporate RFx was fabricated.
gaps: CRITICAL: WebFetch is denied by the session outbound egress policy for ALL hosts. Every WebFetch call returned HTTP 403 on the CONNECT tunnel, confirmed against control hosts example.com, google.com and en.wikipedia.org (all 403), not just tender sites. The proxy status endpoint recorded 'gateway answered 403 to CONNECT (policy denial or upstream failure)' for tenders.gov.au, tenders.vic.gov.au, tenders.nsw.gov.au, brisbane.qld.gov.au, vendorpanel.com.au, sydneywater.com.au, melbournewater.com.au, transgrid.com.au and others. Per /root/.ccr/README.md a proxy 403 is an organization egress-policy denial that must be reported and not retried or bypassed; curl through the proxy returned identical 403s. WebSearch was the only functioning web tool. NOTE: yesterday's run (2026-07-02) clearly had WebFetch access, so this is a change in this session's egress policy/allowlist, not a permanent condition.

No tender detail page or specification document could be opened this run. For every tender captured, deliverables_summary, key_deliverables, scope_of_work, requirements, eligibility, evaluation_criteria, documents, contacts, and (in several cases) value/publish_date could not be verified against the notice and are null. All 4 tenders are headline-level only (entries_pending_detail=4); re-run with fetch access to complete a deliverables/detail pass.

Tier-1 state/territory portals — NSW eTendering/buy.nsw, QTenders (QLD) + supply.qld.gov.au, Tenders WA, Tenders SA + Buying4SA, Tenders Tasmania, NT QTOL, Tenders ACT — all unreachable via WebFetch (403). NSW search snippets surfaced only already-known/excluded items (Moss Vale Rd Barrengarry slope stabilisation) or council items on aggregator/TenderLink pages without a clean government detail URL; no fresh non-excluded NSW/QLD/WA/SA/TAS/NT/ACT STATE tender could be captured with confidence.

Tier-2 (councils/GBEs/universities/health): VendorPanel public tenders and individual buyer portals (Sydney Water, Transgrid, Ausgrid, Melbourne Water, Water Corp WA, Brisbane City Council, NSW Health/HealthShare, TenderLink-hosted portals) all 403 via WebFetch. Genuine portal URLs were recovered from the search index and recorded in discovered_sources, but individual opportunities could not be opened or verified.

Tier-3 (corporate): SAP Ariba Discovery/SAP Business Network Discovery (service.ariba.com/Discovery.aw/), BHP find-opportunities, Rio Tinto, Woodside, Fortescue, Santos, and major retail/bank/telco/energy supplier portals all 403 via WebFetch; per search evidence their opportunity detail is login- or invitation-gated. No publicly-visible corporate RFx could be confirmed, so none was recorded as a tender (portals captured in discovered_sources with access_method=portal-registration). Invitation-only corporate RFPs (e.g. management-consultancy panels) remain out of scope and unobservable.

Recency verification is partial: because detail pages could not be opened, 'published/updated in the last 7 days' could only be inferred from snippet-quoted publish dates for the 4 captured ATMs. Some search-index results carried stale/past closing dates; those were excluded.
WebSearch is US-geolocated, which limits freshness/geo-ranking of Australian results.

Energy-network buyers Jemena, AusNet Services and SA Power Networks were identified by name but their supplier/tender portals/platforms could not be confirmed (likely login-gated) — deferred to a future run.
recommended_seed_additions: ICN Gateway (gateway.icn.org.au) — national major-projects / defence work-package marketplace, public browse + free supplier registration

illion TenderLink (illion.tenderlink.com; per-buyer portals at portal.tenderlink.com/<buyer>) — hosts many buyer-originated e-tendering portals (ARTC, Inland Rail, City of Sydney, City of Melbourne, Macquarie University, Ausgrid, Sydney Airport, Main Roads WA)
ARTC (Australian Rail Track Corporation) e-tendering — portal.tenderlink.com/artc/alltenders/
WaterNSW procurement — waternsw.com.au (Workday Strategic Sourcing + NSW eTendering)
SA Water tenders & supplier portal — sawater.com.au (SA Tenders + own supplier portal)
Powerlink Queensland suppliers — powerlink.com.au (VendorPanel / Queensland Procurement Solution)
eProcure (site.eprocure.com.au) — additional local-government coverage beyond VendorPanel
BidContender (bidcontender.com.au) and ConX (conx.io) — construction head-contractor to subcontractor tender platforms complementing EstimateOne
Felix (felix.net) — multi-asset-owner vendor-management/RFQ network (ports, mining, utilities)
JAGGAER Advantage (app.jaggaer.com) — e-sourcing platform used by HealthShare Victoria and other AU health/public-sector buyers
Macquarie University and Australian Catholic University (ACU) procurement portals; University Procurement Hub (hes.edu.au/uph)
City of Sydney, City of Melbourne and University of Technology Sydney (UTS) current-tender listings; NSW Health tenders hub (health.nsw.gov.au) / HealthShare NSW
St Vincent's Health Australia and Ventia (upcoming defence base-services subcontract opportunities)

tenders.title: Wharf 7 and South Wharf remediation works
tenders.category: 72100000 - Building construction and support and maintenance and repair services
tenders.description: The Australian National Maritime Museum is seeking an appropriately qualified organisation to remediate wharf structures forming part of Wharf 7 and the South Wharf, to restore functional stability by replacing severely compromised construction elements and repairing deteriorated elements where feasible.
tenders.reasoning: Surfaced via WebSearch of the live AusTender Current ATM page; real deep-link /Atm/Show/ URL and ATM identity captured from the search index. Buyer-originated (Commonwealth agency publishing on AusTender).
tenders.notes: HEADLINE-LEVEL ONLY. Identity (title, ATM id, deep-link URL, buyer), category and snippet-quoted publish/closing dates were captured from the AusTender page as surfaced by the web-search index; the detail page itself could NOT be opened this run because WebFetch is egress-policy blocked (HTTP 403 on all hosts). Deliverables, requirements, eligibility, evaluation criteria, documents, value and contacts are unverified and null. Re-run with fetch access to complete the detail pass.

tenders.title: Testing laboratories with appropriate accreditation to undertake verification testing of products regulated under the Greenhouse and Energy Minimum Standards Act 2012 (GEMS Act)
tenders.buyer: Department of Climate Change, Energy, the Environment and Water
tenders.description: Seeking accredited testing laboratories to undertake verification testing of products regulated under the Greenhouse and Energy Minimum Standards Act 2012 (GEMS Act), to confirm compliance with mandatory regulatory requirements and verify the accuracy of energy efficiency / performance claims.
tenders.contract_term: Anticipated deed to commence 1 November 2026 and conclude 30 June 2030 (per snippet; unverified)
tenders.reasoning: Surfaced via WebSearch of the live AusTender Current ATM page; real deep-link /Atm/Show/ URL and ATM_2026_6786 identity captured from the search index. Buyer-originated (DCCEEW on AusTender).
tenders.notes: HEADLINE-LEVEL ONLY. Contract start/end and title captured from the AusTender page snippet as surfaced by the web-search index; publish date not visible. Detail page could NOT be opened (WebFetch egress-blocked, HTTP 403). Deliverables, accreditation requirements, eligibility, evaluation criteria and documents unverified and null. Re-run with fetch access to complete the detail pass.

tenders.buyer: Department of Health, Disability and Ageing
tenders.description: Approach to market published by the Department of Health, Disability and Ageing categorised under comprehensive health services. Full title and scope could not be captured because the detail page is not fetchable this run.
tenders.reasoning: Surfaced via WebSearch of the live AusTender Current ATM page; real deep-link /Atm/Show/ URL and ATM id captured from the search index. Buyer-originated (Department of Health, Disability and Ageing on AusTender).
tenders.notes: HEADLINE-LEVEL ONLY and LOW DETAIL. Only the ATM id, deep-link URL, buyer, category and snippet-quoted publish/closing dates were recoverable; the tender TITLE itself was not visible in the search snippet and is null. Detail page could NOT be opened (WebFetch egress-blocked, HTTP 403). Everything else unverified/null. Re-run with fetch access to confirm the title and capture scope.

tenders.title: Maintenance of High Voltage (HV) Electrical assets at Melbourne Market, Epping
tenders.buyer: Melbourne Market Authority (tender managed by BGIS)
tenders.description: BGIS, on behalf of the Melbourne Market Authority, is seeking a professional organisation to maintain the High Voltage (HV) electrical assets at the Melbourne Market site in Epping, Victoria, including reactive services on an as-needed basis.
tenders.briefing: Site visit briefing indicated for 7 July 2026 at 10:00am, 1/55 Produce Drive, Epping (per snippet; unverified, mandatory status unknown)
tenders.submission_method: Lodge via Buying for Victoria portal
tenders.reasoning: Surfaced via WebSearch of the live Buying for Victoria portal; real portal-id deep-link (view?id=324738), title, buyer and site-visit note captured from the search index. Buyer-originated (Melbourne Market Authority / BGIS on the Victorian portal).
tenders.notes: HEADLINE-LEVEL ONLY. Title, buyer, delivery location and a site-visit note were captured from the portal page as surfaced by the web-search index; publish/closing dates were not visible and are null. Detail page could NOT be opened (WebFetch egress-blocked, HTTP 403). Deliverables, requirements, eligibility, evaluation criteria, documents, contacts and closing date unverified/null. Re-run with fetch access to complete the detail pass.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: WebSearch for defence-prime and major-project subcontractor portals; ICN Gateway repeatedly surfaced as where government-funded and private project owners list work packages across defence, construction, mining, oil & gas, rail and manufacturing. Free supplier registration with project alerts and express-interest.
discovered_sources.notes: Buyer/project-originated work packages (incl. defence AIC/GSC programs) but functions partly as a marketplace — pivot to the underlying buyer where possible. Portal URL from search index; not fetch-verified this run (session egress block, HTTP 403). Register a free supplier account for alerts/API.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Rail/transport and council procurement searches surfaced many buyer-originated portals hosted on TenderLink subdomains (portal.tenderlink.com/<buyer>), e.g. ARTC, Inland Rail, City of Sydney, City of Melbourne, Macquarie University, Ausgrid, Sydney Airport, Main Roads WA.
discovered_sources.notes: Platform hosts genuine buyer-originated e-tendering portals as subdomains; treat those buyer subdomains as the sources of truth, not the aggregated illion tender list. Distinct from VendorPanel. Portal URLs from search index; not fetch-verified this run (HTTP 403).

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Rail procurement search; ARTC (Commonwealth-owned) publishes current tenders publicly via its TenderLink subdomain.
discovered_sources.notes: Genuine GBE buyer, not in prior known-source list. Public current-tenders list per search results; page 403'd to WebFetch this run (egress block).

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Rail search; Inland Rail uses TenderLink for EOIs/tenders and direct work-package opportunities on a multi-billion-dollar programme.
discovered_sources.notes: New buyer. Portal URL from search index; not fetch-verified this run (HTTP 403).

discovered_sources.platform: Workday Strategic Sourcing (Scout RFP) + NSW eTendering
discovered_sources.how_discovered: Water-utility search; WaterNSW uses Scout RFP for sourcing/prequalification and advertises on NSW eTendering. Distinct from Sydney Water / Melbourne Water / Water Corp WA (already known).
discovered_sources.notes: New GBE buyer. Portal URL from search index; not fetch-verified this run (HTTP 403).

discovered_sources.platform: SA Water Supplier Portal + SA Tenders and Contracts
discovered_sources.how_discovered: Water-utility search; SA Water publishes RFT/RFQ/RFI on SA Tenders and runs its own Supplier Portal (Initial Bidder Registration).
discovered_sources.notes: New GBE buyer (SA Tenders itself already known). Portal URL from search index; not fetch-verified this run (HTTP 403).

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Energy-network procurement search; Powerlink (QLD electricity transmission GBE) publishes tenders via VendorPanel / Queensland Procurement Solution.
discovered_sources.notes: New GBE buyer (Transgrid/Ausgrid/Energy Queensland already known). Portal URL from search index; not fetch-verified this run (HTTP 403).

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Council tender-platform search; eProcure hosts public tenders for hundreds of councils (~260,000 registered suppliers). Distinct from VendorPanel and TenderLink.
discovered_sources.notes: Multi-buyer local-government platform; complements VendorPanel for council coverage. Portal URL from search index; not fetch-verified this run (HTTP 403).

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Construction-subcontractor platform search; head-contractor-driven tender/RFI distribution giving subcontractors access to construction tenders. Complements EstimateOne (already known).
discovered_sources.notes: Buyer(head-contractor)-originated distribution; invitation/registration-based. Portal URL from search index; not fetch-verified this run (HTTP 403).

discovered_sources.how_discovered: EstimateOne-alternative search; ConX provides cloud estimating + tender management and a subcontractor/builder network.
discovered_sources.notes: Named EstimateOne competitor; subcontractor tender distribution. Portal URL from search index; not fetch-verified this run (HTTP 403).

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Port/utilities procurement search; multiple asset owners (e.g. Mid West Ports) run vendor databases and RFQs via Felix. Registering a vendor profile exposes RFQs from multiple asset-owner clients.
discovered_sources.notes: Multi-buyer vendor-management network; capture the platform, not individual gated RFQs. Portal URL from search index; not fetch-verified this run (HTTP 403).

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Platform-detection search; HealthShare Victoria and Victorian public health services run RFI/RFQ via JAGGAER Advantage. Sibling-buyer angle to find other JAGGAER-hosted AU buyers (health, universities).
discovered_sources.notes: Buyer-specific portals sit behind login; register once to see multiple health buyers' RFx. Capture the platform, not gated RFx. Portal URL from search index; not fetch-verified this run (HTTP 403).

discovered_sources.how_discovered: University procurement search; Macquarie advertises RFTs/EOIs/RFQs via TenderLink. New university buyer (Uni Sydney/JCU/TAFE NSW already known).
discovered_sources.notes: Portal URL from search index; not fetch-verified this run (HTTP 403).

discovered_sources.how_discovered: University procurement search; ACU runs a Tender Portal showing public and open tenders.
discovered_sources.notes: New university buyer. Portal URL from search index; not fetch-verified this run (HTTP 403).

discovered_sources.source_name: University of Technology Sydney (UTS) — Current tenders
discovered_sources.how_discovered: University procurement search; UTS publishes a current-tenders listing page.
discovered_sources.notes: New university buyer. Portal URL from search index; not fetch-verified this run (HTTP 403).

discovered_sources.source_name: University Procurement Hub (HES / Higher Education Services)
discovered_sources.how_discovered: University procurement search; UPH aggregates sourcing/panels across participating Australian universities.
discovered_sources.notes: Panels/aggregated approaches to market across universities; pivot to the participating university buyer where possible. Portal URL from search index; not fetch-verified this run (HTTP 403).

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Council tender search; City of Sydney publishes current tenders on its buyer-originated TenderLink subdomain.
discovered_sources.notes: New local-government buyer (Inner West Council already known). Public listing per search; page 403'd to WebFetch this run (egress block).

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Council tender search; City of Melbourne publishes current tenders on its buyer-originated TenderLink subdomain (also advertised in The Age).
discovered_sources.notes: New local-government buyer. Public listing per search; page 403'd to WebFetch this run (egress block).

discovered_sources.source_name: NSW Health — Health tenders / HealthShare NSW
discovered_sources.how_discovered: Public-health procurement search; central hub for NSW Local Health District goods/services procurement, with HealthShare NSW running shared procurement.
discovered_sources.notes: New health source (routes to NSW eTendering /health sub-portal). Portal URL from search index; not fetch-verified this run (HTTP 403).

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Private/NFP health procurement search; St Vincent's runs group competitive tender processes across its facilities.
discovered_sources.notes: New NFP-health buyer; platform not confirmed (likely login-gated). Portal URL from search index; not fetch-verified this run (HTTP 403).

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Defence prime/subcontract search; Ventia (Tier-1 services contractor) publishes upcoming defence base-services opportunities and subcontractor EOIs.
discovered_sources.notes: Head-contractor subcontract pipeline; complements ICN Gateway for defence. Portal URL from search index; not fetch-verified this run (HTTP 403).
